name: Dana Naser
headline: Data Analyst • SQL • Business Intelligence • KPI Reporting
profile: Data-driven Information Systems student focused on SQL, KPI design, and business reporting. I transform structured data into clear insights that support decision-making.

about.p1: I’m a third-year B.Sc. Information Systems student at the University of Haifa, specializing in data analytics and business intelligence.
about.p2: My strength is translating business questions into structured SQL logic, KPIs, and reporting views that create operational clarity and support better decisions.
what_i_bring: BI mindset: KPIs, trends, anomalies, operational visibility

case_study.title: Winery Sales & Inventory Analysis
case_study.summary: A structured analytics foundation to support KPI tracking and operational decision-making.
case_study.brief: Improve inventory visibility and identify sales & stock risks using normalized data + KPI queries.
before_after: Before: limited KPI visibility and inconsistent reporting logic
After: normalized schema + repeatable SQL KPI queries for monitoring

projects.name: Winery Sales & Inventory Analysis
projects.one_liner: Designed SQL-driven KPI monitoring to improve inventory visibility and decision-making.
projects.problem: Inventory levels were not aligned with demand trends, limiting operational visibility.
projects.objective: Track KPIs and improve inventory awareness to support operational decisions.
approach: Designed ERD and normalized tables for reliable querying
Built analytical SQL queries for KPI tracking
Simulated reporting logic for management insights
insights: Highlighted stock imbalance patterns across items
recommendations: Rebalance low-rotation items to reduce holding risk
Monitor weekly KPI indicators to prevent shortages

projects.name: StockSync
projects.one_liner: Inventory tracking concept focused on clarity, visibility, and operational thinking.
projects.problem: Users struggle to track inventory clearly and identify waste-driving patterns.
projects.objective: Improve inventory awareness and reduce waste through structured visibility.
approach: Designed mobile-friendly UI focused on clarity & speed
Structured inventory logic to enable KPI-style monitoring
Supported recipe suggestions based on available ingredients
insights: Highlights UX best practices for fast decision-making
recommendations: Add simple KPI widgets (items expiring soon, weekly waste)
Include filters for category + consumption rate

projects.name: Hospital Operations System
projects.one_liner: Desktop system emphasizing clean data structure and maintainable process logic.
projects.problem: Operational records and workflows require consistent structure to stay reliable.
projects.objective: Model core entities and workflows to support coordination and maintainability.
approach: Built consistent OOP logic for managing operations & records
Focused on maintainability and structured thinking
recommendations: Add reporting views for operational monitoring
Document workflows and entity relationships clearly
